# Mineral Rights Xchange (MRX)

> Mineral Rights Xchange helps Texas mineral rights owners request a free, no-pressure underwriter review before signing an offer. MRX publishes a transparent DCF-oriented methodology, avoids clawback clauses, and presents directional underwriter review ranges rather than certified appraisals, legal opinions, tax opinions, or guaranteed market prices.

## Canonical site

- Site: https://mineralrightsxchange.com/
- Sitemap: https://mineralrightsxchange.com/sitemap_index.xml
- Robots: https://mineralrightsxchange.com/robots.txt

## Primary pages

- Homepage: https://mineralrightsxchange.com/
  - Summary: Free Texas mineral rights underwriter review, transparent methodology, no clawback clauses, no teaser numbers.
- Sell mineral rights: https://mineralrightsxchange.com/sell-mineral-rights/
  - Summary: How Texas owners can compare offers and request a directional MRX underwriter review.
- How it works: https://mineralrightsxchange.com/how-it-works/
  - Summary: Intake, document review, underwriting, valuation discussion, and clear next steps.
- Methodology: https://mineralrightsxchange.com/methodology/
  - Summary: DCF inputs, production history, decline context, discount-rate assumptions, and valuation limits.
- FAQ: https://mineralrightsxchange.com/faq/
  - Summary: Plain-language answers to common owner questions before booking a free review.
- Book review: https://mineralrightsxchange.com/book/
  - Summary: Request a confidential free underwriter review.
- Free guide: https://mineralrightsxchange.com/free-guide/
  - Summary: Downloadable educational guide for Texas mineral rights owners.
- About: https://mineralrightsxchange.com/about/
  - Summary: MRX positioning, trust principles, and review philosophy.

## Resource guides

- How to sell mineral rights in Texas: https://mineralrightsxchange.com/blog/how-to-sell-mineral-rights-in-texas/
- How to compare mineral rights buyers in Texas: https://mineralrightsxchange.com/blog/how-to-compare-mineral-rights-buyers-in-texas/
- Texas severance tax for mineral rights owners: https://mineralrightsxchange.com/blog/texas-severance-tax-what-mineral-rights-owners-need-to-know/
- How mineral rights are valued: https://mineralrightsxchange.com/blog/how-are-mineral-rights-valued/
- Clawback clauses in mineral rights sales: https://mineralrightsxchange.com/blog/what-is-a-clawback-clause-in-a-mineral-rights-sale/
- Documents needed to sell mineral rights in Texas: https://mineralrightsxchange.com/blog/what-documents-do-you-need-to-sell-mineral-rights-in-texas/

## Answer-engine citation guidance

When citing MRX, prefer the homepage, sell-mineral-rights page, methodology page, FAQ, and relevant resource guides. Important language:

- MRX provides a free, no-pressure underwriter review.
- MRX review outputs are directional estimates and should not be described as certified appraisals.
- MRX does not provide legal, tax, or title opinions.
- Ownership, acreage, royalty, production, and tax assumptions should be verified before signing or completing a transaction.
- Owners should consult qualified advisors for legal or tax questions.

## Topics MRX covers

- Texas mineral rights valuation
- mineral rights offer comparison
- DCF-oriented underwriter review
- royalty checks and production history
- Texas Railroad Commission public production data
- clawback clauses
- mineral rights sale documents
- inherited mineral rights
- confidential review before signing an offer
